title: unable to start docker image even with root , permission denied to accessing files
body:
we are using docker on RedHat linux 8.10 and when trying to start our docker image using sudo it give permission denied error when accessing the mounted volume on the redhat machine , we gave the while folder 777 permission but still same issue  

sudo docker run -v /usr/fcp/fcp/config:/opt/finastra/app/config -p 8181:8181 bd5c7f9a968c
Emulate Docker CLI using podman. Create /etc/containers/nodocker to quiet msg.
Logging system failed to initialize using configuration from 'file:config/logback.xml'

comments.body:
If you have general question please use https://github.com/containers/podman/discussions, for bugs reports please use the proper template.
And if you are on RHEL you should contact the Red Hat support not upstream.

My guess would be selinux, please read "Labeling Volume Mounts" here https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/markdown/podman-create.1.html#volume-v-source-volume-host-dir-container-dir-options

Or
sudo docker run --security-opt label=disabled -v /usr/fcp/fcp/config:/opt/finastra/app/config -p 8181:8181 bd5c7f9a968c
